title: Live Aluminum Prices Today
summary: Aluminum prices retreated 0.69% to $3,526.89 per ton globally and ¥24,064 in China, easing from recent four-year highs due to signals of easing supply pressures. Global prices consolidated at $3,557.12 per ton with a 0.14% decline amid geopolitical supply shocks from maritime blockades in the Strait of Hormuz and strikes on Middle Eastern smelters. Chinese prices remain firm at ¥24,244 per ton, supported by domestic demand and production caps, while depleted LME stocks and high energy costs influence sentiment.

title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Aluminum fell to 3,532.50 USD/T on April 21, 2026, down 0.63% from the previous day, despite concerns over supply disruptions from the Persian Gulf region accounting for 9% of global output. Aluminum futures in the UK retreated toward $3,540 per tonne from a four-year high after Iran re-blocked the Strait of Hormuz, blaming the US. Over the past month, prices rose 9.45%, up 47.52% year-over-year.

title: Aluminum Holds Near 3-Year High
summary: Aluminum futures in the UK fell toward $3,540 per tonne, retreating from a four-year high. China's output is expected to stall after hitting its 45 million ton cap in 2025, while smelters in Iceland, Mozambique, and Australia have suspended operations due to high energy costs and equipment failures.

title: Assan Alüminyum Expands North American Footprint with Aluminum Foil Plant Acquisition
summary: Assan Alüminyum acquired an aluminum foil plant to expand its North American operations. Additionally, Compañía Valenciana de Aluminio Baux installed a new 4-high cold rolling mill in Spain to increase cold rolled aluminum production.

title: Century Aluminum Begins Hot Metal Production at Mt Holly Plant
summary: Century Aluminum has begun producing its first hot metal following the expansion of its Mt Holly plant in South Carolina, marking a significant capacity milestone.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Steel prices fell to 3,129 CNY/T on April 21, 2026, down 0.29% from the previous day, though still 3.71% higher year-over-year. Steel rebar futures climbed above CNY 3,100 per ton to a two-week high, driven by firm seasonal demand and restocking. China Steel Corp. raised domestic prices for next month by NT$1,000 to NT$1,200 per ton, its fifth consecutive increase.

title: HRC Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: HRC Steel rose to 1,107.10 USD/T on April 21, 2026, up 0.46% from the previous day, reaching the highest since January 2024. Over the past 4 weeks, prices gained 4.99%. This reflects positive momentum in hot-rolled coil steel markets.

title: European steel mills signal €50-70/t long product hikes
summary: Italian and German steel mills signaled €50-70/t ($59-82.50/t) increases in long product offers next week at a Dusseldorf event. Rising energy prices are pressuring costs, with German rebar offers at €710/t delivered and Italian mills planning hikes above €50/t. Recent Italian traded prices were around €650/t ex-works.

title: Industry Data - Weekly Steel Production Report
summary: Domestic raw steel production reached 1,848,000 net tons for the week ending April 18, 2026, with a capability utilization rate of 80.0 percent. Production breakdown by districts includes North East: 133k tons, Great Lakes: 525k tons, Midwest: 295k tons, Southern: 823k tons, and Western: 72k tons.

title: Global Excess Steel Capacity Hits 640M Tons, Challenges U.S. Industry in 2026
summary: The American Iron and Steel Institute highlights global excess steel capacity reaching 640 million tons in 2025, posing challenges to the U.S. industry. Exports from overcapacity continue amid numerous new trade remedy cases, as cited from OECD Steel Committee data.

title: Suez Steel Achieves Record-Low Energy Consumption for DRI Production
summary: Suez Steel has achieved a new benchmark in energy efficiency at its direct reduction plant based on ENERGIRON Zero-Reformer technology. This innovation represents a significant advancement in sustainable steel production by reducing energy use in direct reduced iron (DRI) processes.

title: Yukon Iron and Steel Marks Two Years of Record-Breaking Danieli QSP-DUE Plant Operations
summary: Danieli announced that Yukon Iron and Steel has marked two years of record-breaking operations with its Danieli QSP-DUE plant. This milestone highlights ongoing innovations in steel technology for improved efficiency and sustainability.

title: Copper futures slipped below $6 per pound on Tuesday, remaining under pressure as investors stayed cautious ahead of further US-Iran peace negotiations
summary: Copper futures slipped below $6 per pound on Tuesday due to investor caution ahead of US-Iran peace negotiations. The price held steady near $5.5 per pound but is on track for a nearly 10% loss in March, its worst monthly performance since July last year.

title: Chilean miner Codelco’s El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
summary: Chilean miner Codelco’s smelter at its El Teniente mine is set to restart on Thursday following a collapse. This development could impact copper supply from one of the world's largest producers.

title: Copper hits one-month high as US-Iran talk hopes lift metal prices
summary: Copper prices reached a one-month high driven by optimism from US-Iran talks. This positive sentiment lifted industrial metal prices amid broader market trends.

title: Ivanhoe Mines Reports 71,417 Tonnes of Copper in Anode Produced by Kamoa-Kakula in Q1 2026; Recovery Efforts Advancing
summary: Ivanhoe Mines' Kamoa-Kakula smelter in the DRC produced 71,417 tonnes of copper anode in Q1 2026, with Africa's largest copper smelter ramping up to 60% capacity. Sales of 99.7%-pure anodes and high-strength sulphuric acid are improving margins, with first anodes exported via Lobito Railway to Europe. The on-site 500,000 tonnes-per-annum smelter ramp-up benefits from global supply chain disruptions in acid sales.

title: Copper Market News & Forecasts
summary: Copper in concentrate production from Ivanhoe Mines’ Kamoa-Kakula complex fell to 61,906 tonnes in Q1, down 54% year-over-year, prompting evaluation of local third-party purchases to ramp up the smelter amid sulfuric acid shortages. Lundin Mining and BHP's Vicuña joint venture projects 395,000 tonnes annual copper production over 25 years. Strait of Hormuz closure threatens 40,000 t/month copper cathode flows to UAE as alternative ports near capacity.

title: Rising Copper Inventories & China's Smelter Surge Signal Near-Term Market Rebalancing
summary: China, accounting for 60% of global refined copper demand, is expanding smelting capacity with monthly output nearing 1.2 million tonnes, contributing to rising inventories over 500,000 tonnes and short-term price correction. Despite near-term rebalancing, long-term supply constraints from 17-year mine development timelines and declining ore grades forecast a 30% global copper deficit by 2035.

title: Indonesian Nickel Processors Face Output Cuts Amid Benchmark Price Revision
summary: Indonesian nickel processors are facing output cuts due to a benchmark price revision. This development is part of broader efforts to manage supply amid market pressures. The move could impact global nickel availability and prices.
